steps.title: Spot the appositive and its requirement
steps.content: ”A politician from South Carolina,” is an appositive phrase that renames Robert B. Elliott. The signal: an appositive must be immediately followed by the noun it describes as the main subject of the independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the appositive rule
steps.content:
(A) places Elliott as the subject of the clause immediately after the appositive, satisfying the rule. (B) is a fragment with no subject, while (C) and (D) place different subjects after the appositive.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
